name: Sangreal Full
description: The Sangreal Full, often referred to as the Holy Grail in its state of completion, is not merely a cup or vessel but a symbol of ultimate spiritual grace, divine power, and the fulfillment of earthly and spiritual desires. Its origins are shrouded in mystery, with some accounts linking it to the cup used by Jesus Christ at the Last Supper, while others suggest it was used to collect his blood at the Crucifixion. The Sangreal Full possesses the power to grant eternal life, provide sustenance, heal the sick, and reveal divine truths to those who are pure of heart. It is the ultimate object of the Grail Quest, sought after by many knights of the Round Table, most notably Sir Galahad, Sir Percival, and Sir Bors. Its presence brings prosperity and blessings to the land, while its absence leads to barrenness and decay. The Sangreal Full represents the culmination of spiritual yearning and the possibility of achieving divine union. Its true nature is often veiled in allegory, representing the inner self and the journey towards enlightenment. The Grail's appearance and disappearance are often linked to the moral state of the kingdom of Logres. Only those who are spiritually prepared and morally upright can witness its glory and benefit from its power. The Sangreal Full is a transformative artifact, capable of changing the lives of those who encounter it and the fate of the world around them.
subtitle: The Vessel of Divine Grace
material: Unknown, possibly celestial or divine origin
origin: Various accounts link it to the Last Supper or the Crucifixion of Jesus Christ.
currentLocation: Unknown, believed to reside in a mystical realm or hidden sanctuary.

keyMyths.title: The Grail Quest
keyMyths.description: The quest for the Sangreal is a central theme in Arthurian legend, representing a spiritual journey and the search for divine grace. Knights embark on perilous adventures, facing trials of faith and morality to prove their worthiness.
keyMyths.source: Le Morte d'Arthur by Thomas Malory

keyMyths.title: The Last Supper
keyMyths.description: Some accounts identify the Sangreal as the cup used by Jesus Christ at the Last Supper, imbuing it with divine significance. This connection links the Grail to the central tenets of Christianity and the sacrifice of Christ.

sources.author: Thomas Malory
sources.description: A foundational text for Arthurian legend, detailing the Grail Quest.
sources.text: A foundational text for Arthurian legend, detailing the Grail Quest.

sources.author: Chrétien de Troyes
sources.work: Perceval, the Story of the Grail
sources.description: One of the earliest Grail romances, exploring the themes of chivalry and spiritual awakening.
